  • Portal Particle Acceleration In Jets

Diffusive shock acceleration (DSA)

Diffusive shock acceleration

Type:Mechanism / Concept
Educational level:Intermediate
Key Papers:Morlino et al.; Bell (2011)

DSA is a first-order Fermi process in which particles gain energy by repeatedly crossing a shock and scattering off magnetic turbulence on either side. The statistical energy gain per cycle leads to a power-law spectrum whose index depends on the shock compression ratio.

How it works

  • Scattering — Magnetic turbulence scatters particles, isotropizing their directions locally and allowing repeated shock crossings.
  • Energy gain — Each crossing yields a fractional energy gain proportional to the shock speed in the plasma frame.
  • Spectral index — For strong non-relativistic shocks the canonical index is ~2 (in energy), modified by obliquity and relativistic effects.

Why this matters

2603.16647 solves a spatially-dependent transport equation for particles accelerated at recollimation shocks using DSA boundary conditions. The paper shows how diffusion regime and shock obliquity alter cutoff shapes and maximum energies; see [[particle-acceleration-oblique-shocks]] and [[recollimation-shocks]].

Key references

  1. Bell 2011 — review on DSA and magnetic field amplification.
  2. Morlino et al. — transport-method precedents used in 2603.16647.

Related concepts

  • Particle acceleration (basics)
  • Turbulence & diffusion

See also
